channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Currie Cup Opening Round Highlights: Teams Secure Victories in Competitive Starts
item.description: The opening round of the Currie Cup delivered thrilling results as multiple teams secured victories, showcasing intense competition. The Boland Kavaliers triumphed 24-10 over the Bulls in a muddy Well...

item.title: Andy Burnham's Vision for British Politics: Humble Leadership and Radical Reform
item.description: The article explores Andy Burnham's potential impact as the new UK Prime Minister, highlighting his empathetic approach, humble nature, and commitment to political reform. It references his experience...

item.title: Ireland's Sports Tech Innovations in Soccer
item.description: The article discusses Ireland's advancements in sports technology, particularly focusing on soccer innovations. It highlights how Irish companies are developing cutting-edge equipment and training met...

item.title: U.S.-Iran Conflict Escalates with Airstrikes and Missile Threats
item.description: The United States launched airstrikes in Iran on Sunday in response to the killing of U.S. troops, escalating tensions between the two nations. Iran retaliated by firing missiles toward Jordan, riskin...

item.title: Lagos man raises concern over wide price differences for the same prescription medicine across pharmacies
item.description: A Nigerian man has stirred serious conversation online after sharing his experience of buying the same prescription medicine at two different pharmacies in Lagos for prices that were far apart. Accord...

item.title: England secures third place at the 2026 FIFA World Cup with a 6-4 win over France after surviving a late comeback
item.description: England claimed third place at the 2026 FIFA World Cup with a dramatic 6-4 victory over France in one of the highest-scoring matches in tournament history. Thomas Tuchel's heavily rotated side produce...

item.title: Equity Bank expands agency banking to boost financial inclusion in Tanzania
item.description: Equity Bank Tanzania is increasing its investment in agency banking to expand access to financial services in underserved communities. The bank recently held a capacity-building forum in Dar es Salaam...

item.title: Tanzania and Egypt boost collaboration on key development initiatives
item.description: The article discusses the commitment of Egypt and Tanzania to accelerate joint strategic projects aimed at enhancing bilateral cooperation. The initiative focuses on infrastructure development, trade ...

item.title: US-Iran conflict escalates with airstrikes and regional tensions
item.description: The United States has launched airstrikes against Iran's Revolutionary Guard in retaliation for the killing of two American service members in Jordan, marking a significant escalation in the regional ...

item.title: Amy Shark Reflects on Fame and Artistry with New Album 'Soft Pop'
item.description: Amy Shark, the Gold Coast singer known for her breakout hit 'Adore', discusses the challenges of fame and artistic evolution in her fourth album 'Soft Pop'. After a decade in the industry, Shark ackno...

item.title: Canada implements travel restrictions for foreign nationals visiting Congo amid Ebola outbreak
item.description: Canada has introduced new travel restrictions to prevent the spread of Ebola, barring foreign nationals who have visited the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC) in the past 21 days from entering the co...

item.title: National Government Allocates Extra P20 Million for Rehabilitation of Earthquake-Hit Areas in South Cotabato
item.description: The national government has pledged an additional P20 million in financial assistance to support the rehabilitation efforts for communities affected by the earthquake in South Cotabato province. This ...

item.title: Church Congregation Forced To Worship Outdoors Due To Flood Crisis
item.description: A severe flood has submerged the New Covenant Anglican Church in Ogale Parish, Eleme Local Government Area, Rivers State, forcing members to hold Sunday worship services outdoors. The flood, caused by...

item.title: Trainspotter fractures spine after falling from historic Big Boy locomotive during US tour
item.description: A trainspotter has suffered a spinal fracture after falling from the world's largest locomotive, Big Boy 4014, during its cross-United States journey. The incident occurred as the iconic steam engine,...

item.title: School fees strain families, with grandparents stepping in to help
item.description: New research reveals that over half of families with children at private schools or considering enrollment face significant financial pressure due to rising education costs. Independent Schools Austra...

item.title: Zanzibar Higher Education Exhibition Encourages Form Six Graduates to Explore University Opportunities
item.description: Form Six graduates in Zanzibar are being encouraged to attend the ongoing Zanzibar Higher Education Week Exhibition to gain guidance on university admissions, scholarships, and student loans. The even...

item.title: Heavy rainfall causes destruction of homes and farmlands in Plateau State
item.description: Heavy rainfall hit the Darwat-Jol community in Riyom Local Government Area of Plateau State, causing massive destruction. Over 20 houses were destroyed, displacing many families. The torrential downpo...

item.title: Man accused of killing wife in Delhi arrested after three-day chase
item.description: A man accused of killing his wife in Delhi's Seelampur was arrested after a three-day manhunt. The victim, a 45-year-old woman, was found dead in her home with severe injuries. Her husband, 52-year-ol...

item.title: Zulu King Finalizes Lobola Negotiations for Fourth Wife, Princess Sihle Mdluli
item.description: King Misuzulu kaZwelithini has officially concluded lobola negotiations for his fourth wife, Princess Sihle Mdluli, marking a significant step towards their anticipated wedding later this year. The ce...

item.title: Canada imposes temporary travel ban on foreigners who visited Congo amid Ebola outbreak
item.description: The Canadian government has implemented a temporary travel restriction, barring foreign nationals who have been in the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC) within the past 21 days from entering Canada. ...
